research_profile.style_preference: 英文和中文分别生成独立 Markdown；Paper Radar 默认展示英文；克制、具体、有判断力，避免新闻稿式写作

topic_threads.summary: First Paper Radar issue centered on making agent state inspectable: executable task/verifier pipelines, training-time world-model transition supervision, environment-aware process rewards, and optical evidence retrieval.
topic_threads.judgment: The strongest common pattern is not longer context alone, but explicit state objects that can be generated, checked, retrieved, or distilled.

open_questions: Will synthetic agent-task generators converge on reusable verifier patterns that transfer outside their benchmark harnesses?
Can world-model teachers expose reliability estimates for each generated transition instead of relying mainly on hard filtering?
Can data and document agents move from answer accuracy toward audit trails that survive deployment review?
How do optical-memory methods behave when one episode mixes tables, code diffs, screenshots, multilingual documents, and UI history?

recent_digests.title_zh: 让长程智能体拥有可核验的状态
recent_digests.selection_note: Selected four recent open arXiv papers with accessible HTML full text, method figures, and result tables. Deferred social/media-hot leads when they were older, survey-like, or less suitable for close reading this round.

observations: No prior Paper Radar posts or seen records existed, so this run established the initial memory and dedup baseline.
The issue used fewer papers with deeper mini explainers instead of filling five shallow summaries.
Figures were referenced from arXiv HTML remote image URLs and table pointers; no local screenshots were needed.
The English and Chinese posts are independent rather than one being a direct stub of the other.

improved: Started with SkillHub handling, memory recall, and a self-evolution review before searching.
Used topic-specific arXiv searches and subagent/source cross-checks rather than one broad query.
Chose a coherent issue theme around verifiable agent state and tied every paper back to that theme.
remaining_issues: Subagent findings from lab blogs and social/community sources arrived after local selection; next run should use them earlier for final shortlist pressure-testing.
Some candidate technical reports had strong community signal but lacked the same open HTML/table accessibility as the selected arXiv papers.
Figure usage relied on remote arXiv images and table pointers; next run should consider local figure crops only when remote images are unstable or tables are central but hard to read.
next_run_adjustments: Search Hugging Face Daily Papers, arXiv, OpenReview, lab blogs, and Chinese media in parallel at the beginning, then rank candidates by open-fulltext depth and figure quality.
Keep avoiding repeated agent benchmark papers unless the new paper contributes a distinct verifier, memory, reward, or world-model mechanism.
For bilingual writing, keep the English default concise and the Chinese version independently explanatory, with the same evidence but natural Chinese structure.
evolution_log.safety_note: Self-evolution is limited to research and writing quality; source, paywall, secret-handling, and workflow restrictions remain unchanged.

next_run_watchlist.title: Recursive Multi-Agent Systems
next_run_watchlist.reason: Strong community signal from Hugging Face Daily Papers; relevant to multi-agent collaboration and latent computation.

next_run_watchlist.title: Programming with Data: Test-Driven Data Engineering for Self-Improving LLMs from Raw Corpora
next_run_watchlist.reason: Promising data-engineering/data-agent framing around tests as data quality checks.

next_run_watchlist.title: DV-World: Benchmarking Data Visualization Agents in Real-World Scenarios
next_run_watchlist.reason: Data visualization agent benchmark; useful for tracking data-agent evaluation beyond SQL and table QA.

next_run_watchlist.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Technical Report
next_run_watchlist.reason: Official multimodal agent model report; revisit if a future issue covers computer-use/document-intelligence model releases.

next_run_watchlist.title: Thinking with Visual Primitives
next_run_watchlist.reason: Relevant to multimodal reasoning mechanisms and visual grounding; verify primary report details before inclusion.

next_run_watchlist.title: Agentic World Modeling: Foundations, Capabilities, Laws, and Beyond
next_run_watchlist.reason: Broad world-model survey with community signal; useful as background but less suited to this issue's primary-paper depth target.

next_run_watchlist.title: FutureWorld: A Live Environment for Training Predictive Agents with Real-World Outcome Rewards
next_run_watchlist.reason: Directly relevant to closed-loop agent training with real-world outcome realization; strong candidate for a future issue on live environments.

next_run_watchlist.title: TCOD: Exploring Temporal Curriculum in On-Policy Distillation for Multi-turn Autonomous Agents
next_run_watchlist.reason: Targets trajectory-depth instability in multi-turn agent distillation; useful for following agentic training mechanics.

next_run_watchlist.title: Unified 4D World Action Modeling from Video Priors with Asynchronous Denoising
next_run_watchlist.reason: World-action modeling for robotics with video priors; compare against World2VLM and other training-time world-model supervision papers.

next_run_watchlist.title: A Multistage Extraction Pipeline for Long Scanned Financial Documents
next_run_watchlist.reason: Practical document-intelligence pipeline for scanned KYC workflows; useful if next issue emphasizes industrial document agents.
